title: Database Interface Explorer — Companion Notes
subtitle: Ch. 2 · Introduction to Legal Research Platforms
intro: Fill this in as you click through the simulated platform. The goal is a map you can carry to Westlaw, Lexis, or any other database: every platform arranges the same furniture, just in different rooms.

fields.label: Plain-language search vs. terms and connectors — what does each control, and when would I use each?
Browse by source type — when do I already know what kind of law I need?
Federal vs. state collections — what tells me which one governs my question?
Practice-area pages — what do they gather in one place, and when is that the right door in?
Litigation analytics — what can it tell me, and what should I stay skeptical about?
Legal writing assistant — what does it draft, and what do I still verify myself?
Other platforms in my subscription — which ones, and what is each one for?
Folders / workspace — how do I keep one matter's research together?
History — when will I need to retrace my steps?
Live chat and reference support — when is asking the fastest move?

footer: Every platform is the same house with the furniture moved. Learn the furniture, not the floor plan.
